codeReview.summary: The implementation satisfies the feature requirements: `load_all` and `bulk_insert_videos` were parallelized with `JoinSet` + bounded `Semaphore` (`MAX_CONCURRENT_S3_OPS = 12`), channel snapshot loading now reuses one loaded video slice per channel, and `get_video` now supports skipping summary fetch when not needed. Error paths in parallel sections return `StoreError` rather than dropping failures.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture notes are now stale for this area: they still describe sequential `load_all`, sequential `bulk_insert_videos`, unconditional summary fetch in `get_video`, and duplicate channel snapshot loading, all of which were changed by this feature.

The rust-backend-worker skill enforces strict RED-first failing tests, but this feature relied on a mix of pure unit tests plus ignored S3 integration tests and did not run a dedicated failing-test phase after adding tests. The skill likely needs an explicit exception for external-service-dependent backend paths.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton and commit 59e557f for backend-parallel-s3-ops. No blocking issues found; feature behavior matches mission expectations. Logged shared-state observations for stale architecture notes and a skill-procedure mismatch around RED-first testing for S3-dependent work.
